criteria.reason: Resolver and production role smoke prevent scope target substitution.
Core workspaces use real domain queues and persisted history.
Reviews support filters, list/count parity and governed draft actions.
Cards, content and services are native and honest about read-only capabilities.
Finance and partnerships expose real scope-filtered queues without unsafe sends or payments.
AI employee and latest run state are visible; ungoverned paid launch is not exposed.
Settings persist per scope; diagnostics requires platform resolution.
Manifest and deep-link gates hide unsupported modules.
Telegram remains Mini-App-first with server-validated deep links.
Responsive mobile UX and frontend build checks pass.
13 targeted and 159 regression tests pass, plus three-role production smoke.
GitHub/GitVerse push, partial deploy and live smoke succeeded.
